<!-- ---
title: "Analysis of Data – Project 2"
author: "200120178"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
    highlight: tango
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
--- -->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
    echo    = TRUE,
    warning = FALSE,
    message = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
library(gplots)
library(GGally)
library(ggplot2)
library(corrplot)
```

## 1  Data Loading

```{r load-data}
# Read the exam marks data
# Row 1 contains the max. possible marks; rows 2+ are student records
raw <- read.table(
    "200120178exammarks.dat",
    header    = TRUE, # first line holds column names (A1 A2 ... sum)
    row.names = 1 # first column holds row labels (student IDs / max.)
)

# Separate the maximum marks row from the student data
max_marks <- raw["max.", ]
marks <- raw[rownames(raw) != "max.", ]

# Quick sanity check
cat("Number of students:", nrow(marks), "\n")
cat("Columns:", paste(colnames(marks), collapse = ", "), "\n")
```

```{r preview}
head(marks)
```

```{r structure}
# Drop the sum column — only analyse individual question marks
marks_q <- marks[, colnames(marks) != "sum"]

str(marks_q)
summary(marks_q)
```

---

## 2  Exploratory Analysis

### 2.1  Histograms per question

```{r histograms, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
par(mfrow = c(2, 4))
for (q in names(marks_q)) {
    qmax <- as.numeric(max_marks[[q]])
    hist(marks_q[[q]],
        breaks = seq(-0.5, qmax + 0.5, by = 1),
        main = q, xlab = "Marks", col = "lightblue", xaxt = "n"
    )
    axis(1, at = 0:qmax)
}
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```

### 2.2  Boxplot

```{r boxplot, fig.width=9, fig.height=5}
boxplot(marks_q,
    main = "Distribution of marks by question",
    col = c(rep("skyblue", 4), rep("tomato", 4))
)
```

### 2.3  Heatmap

```{r heatmap}
# Custom clustering: columns by correlation distance, rows by Euclidean on scaled data
markcor <- cor(marks_q)
cordist <- 0.5 - markcor / 2
colclust <- hclust(as.dist(cordist), method = "average")
rowclust <- hclust(dist(scale(marks_q)), method = "average")

# Blue → white → red diverging palette (101 steps)
bwr_pal <- colorRampPalette(c("#2166ac", "#f7f7f7", "#d6604d"))(101)

# Plot heatmap
heatmap.2(as.matrix(marks_q),
    labRow = rownames(marks_q),
    Rowv = as.dendrogram(rowclust),
    Colv = as.dendrogram(colclust),
    scale = "col", # scale each question for comparability
    trace = "none", # no trace lines
    col = bwr_pal,
    key.title = "Std. score",
    density.info = "histogram"
)
```

### 2.4  Jitter plot

```{r jitterplot, fig.width=9, fig.height=6}
# Reshape to long format: one row per (student, question, mark)
questions <- colnames(marks_q)
q_index <- rep(seq_along(questions), each = nrow(marks_q))
mark_vec <- unlist(marks_q)

# Jitter plot — reveals distribution of discrete marks per question
plot(jitter(q_index), jitter(mark_vec),
    xlab = "Question", ylab = "Mark",
    xaxt = "n", # suppress default x-axis
    pch = 16, cex = 0.7, # solid small dots
    col = adjustcolor("steelblue", alpha.f = 0.5),
    main = "Student marks by question (jittered)"
)

axis(1, at = seq_along(questions), labels = questions)

# Add max-mark reference lines for each question
segments(
    x0 = seq_along(questions) - 0.4,
    x1 = seq_along(questions) + 0.4,
    y0 = as.numeric(max_marks[questions]),
    col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2
)

legend("topright", legend = "Max. mark", col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 2, bty = "n")
```

### 2.5  Parallel coordinates

Note that the lines have 30% opacity, so that when they overlap they dont completely obscure each other.

```{r parcoord, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# Create performance groups based on total mark terciles
marks_pc <- marks_q
marks_pc$total <- marks$sum
breaks <- quantile(marks_pc$total, probs = c(0, 1 / 3, 2 / 3, 1))
marks_pc$performance <- cut(marks_pc$total,
    breaks = breaks,
    labels = c("Low", "Mid", "High"),
    include.lowest = TRUE
)

# Parallel coordinates plot — map linewidth to performance group
ggparcoord(marks_pc,
    columns     = seq_along(questions), # question columns only
    groupColumn = "performance",
    # scale       = "uniminmax", # scale each axis to [0, 1]
    alphaLines  = 0.3
) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(
        "Low"  = "#d6604d",
        "Mid"  = "#4393c3",
        "High" = "#1a9850"
    )) +
    labs(
        title = "Parallel coordinates – marks by question",
        x     = "Question",
        y     = "Scaled mark (0 = min, 1 = max)",
        color = "Performance"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```

### 2.6  Correlation matrix

```{r correlation}
cor_mat <- cor(marks_q)
round(cor_mat, 2)
```

```{r corrplot, fig.width=7, fig.height=7}
corrplot(cor_mat,
    method       = "color", # coloured heatmap
    type         = "upper", # only upper triangle (cleaner)
    addCoef.col  = "black", # add correlation numbers
    tl.col       = "black", # text label colour
    number.cex   = 0.8, # size of the numbers
    tl.cex       = 0.8
)
```

### 2.7  Pairs plot

```{r pairs, fig.width=10, fig.height=10}
pairs(marks_q, main = "Pairs plot of exam questions")
```

---

## 3  PCA

```{r pca}
pca <- prcomp(marks_q, scale. = TRUE)

summary(pca) # % variance explained
pca$rotation # loadings
```

```{r scree}
plot(pca, type = "l", main = "Scree plot")
```

```{r biplot, fig.width=8, fig.height=8}
biplot(pca, scale = 0)
```

---

## 4  Section Comparison (A vs B)

```{r section-means}
A_mean <- apply(marks_q[, 1:4], 2, mean)
B_mean <- apply(marks_q[, 5:8], 2, mean)

A_mean
B_mean
```

```{r section-ttest}
t.test(rowSums(marks_q[, 1:4]), rowSums(marks_q[, 5:8]))
```

```{r a-vs-b, fig.width=7, fig.height=6}
marks_q$A_total <- rowSums(marks_q[, 1:4])
marks_q$B_total <- rowSums(marks_q[, 5:8])

plot(marks_q$A_total, marks_q$B_total,
    xlab = "Total A marks", ylab = "Total B marks",
    pch = 19, col = "darkblue",
    main = "A vs B marks per student"
)
abline(lm(B_total ~ A_total, data = marks_q), col = "red")

# Clean up: remove temporary columns so marks_q stays clean for later use
marks_q$A_total <- NULL
marks_q$B_total <- NULL
```

---

## 5  Linear Model

```{r lm-summary}
# Fit a linear model: sum ~ A1 + A2 + A3 + A4 + B1 + B2 + B3 + B4
model <- lm(sum ~ A1 + A2 + A3 + A4 + B1 + B2 + B3 + B4, data = marks)
summary(model)
```
